scope.summary: Document and implement the Alembic import shim (Python/Blender-headless read + OBJ-sequence conversion) so Tailor can ingest pre-simulated cloth caches from Houdini and Maya as GarmentFrame sequences
acceptance_criteria: The operator and model documentation (ModelManual entry) states that native Alembic read is deferred; the supported ingest path is: open the .abc in Blender headless, export as numbered OBJ sequence, then POST the sequence to /tailor/garments/:id/import_frames

POST /tailor/garments/:id/import_frames accepts a directory of numbered OBJ files (same naming convention as OBJ sequence export) and stores them as a tailor_animations row with source='alembic_via_obj'
Stored frames are scrub-playable via the viewport (MT-295) and exportable back out via the glTF morph or USD path
A Blender Python script template is provided in docs/recipes/alembic_to_tailor.py that automates the conversion step with no operator-written Python required
scope.risk_if_missed: Alembic is the primary DCC interchange for pre-simulated cloth in VFX pipelines (Houdini, Maya). Without any ingest path, Tailor cannot receive simulation results from upstream DCC tools, blocking the reverse pipeline direction (DCC -> Tailor for look-dev and re-export).

risks: Broad MT scope can hide multiple review targets.
Markdown MT lists may disagree with packet-declared active/next MT truth.
Receipt routing may target an old MT if contract identity is omitted.
minimum_controls: Exactly one primary proof target per MT unless the packet records why not.
MT identity must be stable and referenced by receipt correlation.
Generated projection drift must not wake Coder or Validator by itself.
